symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent flexural nodules with sinus tracts, scarring and double-comedones

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sinus tracts and double-comedones in a recurrent flexural pattern define HS.
A simple abscess lacks chronic sinuses/double-comedones/recurrence.
Absence of advancing erythema/fever makes superimposed infection unlikely now.
Smoking is a major driver of HS severity/flares.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinical diagnosis; swab draining sinus if superinfection suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: HS is clinical; swab only if secondary infection suspected.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Dermatology referral for staging and advanced therapy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Long-term immunomodulatory management (e.g., biologics) for moderate-severe HS.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Blind incision & drainage of diffuse disease
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Often inappropriate/ineffective in diffuse HS; worsens scarring — reserve targeted procedures for specialists.
